stated_strategic_priorities: Scale third-party fee-earning AUM across structured and private credit strategies, leveraging 30+ years of European credit origination expertise
Diversify into private equity secondaries and infrastructure to reduce concentration in credit-linked strategies and broaden the fee base
Expand wealth-channel distribution through the strategic partnership with Amundi, accessing continental European private banks and retail platforms at scale

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: ICG operates across the ~$1.7T global private credit market with particular strength in European mid-market direct lending (~$300B addressable). The European private credit opportunity set remains structurally under-penetrated relative to the US, with bank retrenchment from leveraged lending creating sustained deal flow for non-bank lenders. The firm's structured capital and mezzanine heritage positions it in the ~$200B global subordinated-capital niche where origination barriers to entry are higher than in senior direct lending.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Single-class share structure (LSE premium listing) with standard UK Corporate Governance Code compliance. Board independence consistent with FTSE 250 norms. The 2025 rebrand from Intermediate Capital Group to ICG reflects the firm's evolution beyond its mezzanine-finance origins. Long-tenured CEO Benoît Durteste (appointed 2014, previously CIO) has overseen the transformation from a mezzanine-focused balance-sheet lender to a diversified third-party fund management platform.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: FCA-regulated as a UK alternative investment fund manager (AIFM). Subject to EU AIFMD passporting for continental European fundraising (post-Brexit via equivalence and reverse solicitation). UK Solvency II treatment of private credit favourable for insurance-channel distribution. The Amundi partnership provides EU-domiciled distribution infrastructure, mitigating Brexit-related cross-border friction.
